title: Bypass receive() reverts by dynamically deploying a SELFDESTRUCT envelope
solidity_before: No Solidity equivalent; requires deploying a separate mock contract and calling it.
yul_optimized:
mstore(0x00, to)
mstore8(0x0b, 0x73) // PUSH20
mstore8(0x20, 0xff) // SELFDESTRUCT
create(amount, 0x0b, 0x16)
explanation: Constructs exactly 22 bytes of EVM initialization bytecode in memory. `0x73` is the opcode for `PUSH20`. It pushes the 20-byte target `to` address onto the stack. `0xff` is the opcode for `SELFDESTRUCT`. By calling `create` with the `amount` of ETH as the endowment, the EVM spins up the contract, immediately runs the init code (which self-destructs), and forces the ETH to the target address without triggering any fallback functions.
when_to_apply: Preventing Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks in withdrawal queues, auctions, or royalty payouts.
when_not_to_apply: If the destination requires state changes (like updating an internal ledger) upon receiving ETH.
